key_themes: Strong core growth in trips, bookings, and advertising
Margin expansion, FCF growth, and pricing tailwinds for 2026
Consensus analyst Buy ratings with $103–$107 average targets
summary: As of mid-April 2026, sentiment toward UBER is solidly positive. The stock trades near $77 after a pullback, but analysts widely view it as undervalued with Buy ratings and price targets averaging around $105, citing 20%+ bookings growth, improving profitability, falling insurance costs being reinvested into ride pricing, and strategic AV partnerships in markets like Dubai and Los Angeles.[[1]](https://seekingalpha.com/article/4891002-uber-favorable-ride-pricing-tailwinds-for-2026-underlie-a-cheap-ebitda-multiple)[[2]](https://public.com/stocks/uber/forecast-price-target) Investor discussions on X are somewhat polarized around AV disruption risks (e.g. Tesla/Waymo) versus Uber’s aggregation platform strength, yet many highlight the compelling risk/reward at current multiples with strong FCF inflection expected. Q1 2026 earnings are due May 6, with guidance reflecting growth investments that may pressure near-term EPS but support longer-term momentum.[[3]](https://finance.yahoo.com/markets/stocks/articles/uber-quarterly-earnings-preview-know-073308992.html)
sources: 25+ web news/analyst articles (Yahoo Finance, Seeking Alpha, Uber IR, Trefis, etc.), 20+ recent X posts from investors and analysts (Mar–Apr 2026)
